rationale: The two papers are tightly aligned on multimedia advertising effectiveness: the citing study explicitly says it “extends prior multimedia advertising research,” while the cited paper develops a core dynamic multimedia synergy model and empirical test. Given this conceptual overlap (media interaction/synergy, carryover, media allocation implications), the citation could be foundational or a direct theoretical benchmark rather than a perfunctory mention. With only DOI-level match evidence and no contrary signal, it should be retained for full relation judgment.
